title: dubautia imbricata subsp. acronaea
intro: Dubautia imbricata subsp. acronaea, also known as the Hawaiian tree hibiscus, is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family. The plant has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of Dubautia imbricata subsp. acronaea include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, salves, and tinctures that can be used to treat skin conditions, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
